free_text: Measurements of the neutron energy spectrum of the
spontaneous fission of Cf-252
Main reference. Graphs for
neutron spectra and data for average energies
Numerical values.
Spontaneous fission of Cf-252 encapsulated
in a thin wall container giving 3.E+5 fission events
per minute
Energy spectra were measured using nano-
second time-of-flight technique.
Neutron energy spectra of the spontaneous fission
process of 252Cf were measured in two experiments using
the time-of-flight technique.
NE224 liquid scintillator, 5 cm diameter and
3.8 cm length for neutron detection.
.The relative efficiency of this detector was measured
in the energy region from 0.05 to 8.2 MeV relative to
the known angular distributions of the reactions:
T(p,n)3He, D(d,n)3He, 9Be(alpha,n)12C, H(n,el)H,
and 7Li(p,n)7Be at zero angle.
A second branch from 12.4 to 19.6 MeV was obtained
relative to the T(d,n)4He angular distribution. These
measurements were performed at the pulsed 3 MV Van de
Graaff accelerator of the CBNM. The different parts of
the efficiency curve were normalized to each other in
overlapping regions, while the gap could be easily
filled by extrapolating the two branches.
It is believed, that the relative efficiency of the
detector is known to about 2% below 2.5 MeV and above
this energy with increasing uncertainty
up to 5% at 19 MeV
The neutron detector was the same in both experiments,
whereas the detector which gave the time signal when a
spontaneous fission event occurred, differed in the two
experiments (see Subents 2,3).
-- Reactions for detector efficiency definition.
No correction was applied for delayed neutrons.
.Errors, if present, given in proper SUBENTs.
.Energy uncertainty of each data point were derived
from the time uncertainty(+-1.5 ns).
Date is corrected to 8 digit format, upper
case comments converted to lower case
Last checking has been done.
SD: Information from INC-SOURCE moved to
SAMPLE; FACILITY, MONITOR and MONIT-REF were deleted;
information moved to DETECTOR - it is used for detector
efficiency definition.
Averaged over
data in SUBENT 20401005
.The combined result of the two maxwellian fits  and
two direct averaging of data obtained by start of the
experiments by fission gamma rays and fission
fragments
Given in the initial compilation
From text of Atomkernenergie, vol.22, page 84,
REACTION codes ",PR,AKE,N" changed to
"0-NN-1,PR,KE,,AV,DERIV".
Reference quantity not
given
.Exp. 1. Start using gamma rays from fission products.
.The detector to give the time signal is NE102A
plastic scintillator for gamma ray as timing signal,
Neutrons, gammas. Gamma rays emitted in the
fission process were used to generate a timing
signal to indicate the instant of fission.
.No clear deviation was found from a maxwellian form,
N(E) = C*SQRT(E)*exp(-E/T).
Includes statistical error and systematic
uncertainties due to timing and efficiency errors
Energy errors were derived from the flight
time uncertainty(+-1.5 ns).
Data from H.Liskien as private communication
to a CCDN mission, 1973-10-14
Exp. 2.  Starts using
fission products signal.
2 plastic scintillator foils of 0.2 mm thickness
for fission product detection as timing signal.
Neutron, fission fragments. Fission fragment
as time zero signal.
.No clear deviation was found from a maxwellian form,
N(E) = C*SQRT(E)*exp(-E/T).
Includes statistical error and systematic
uncertainties due to timing and efficiency errors
Energy errors were derived from the flight
time uncertainty(+-1.5 ns).
Data from H.Liskien as private communication
to a CCDN mission, 1973-10-14
.Measurements start by using gamma rays signal from
fission event.
.Measurements start by fission products signal
.Maxwellian fitting of the experimental data
N(E) = C*SQRT(E)*exp(-E/T).
Include errors due to consistency between
data points and fit, systematic uncertainties
due to timing and efficiency errors
From the text in Atomkernenergie, vol.22,
